response: The longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues, Gregg Popovich, was replaced in 1996 by Frank Beamer. Popovich took over as head coach of the San Antonio Spurs in 1996, and was the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues. He was replaced by Frank Beamer, who became the head coach of the Virginia Tech Hokies.
